product.name: Wellness Projection Lab — Franchise / White-Label Multi-Tenancy
product.status: Active Development — Phase A shipped + hardened, Phase B/C planned

changelog.summary: Reconcile PRD with everything that shipped after the original 0.2.0 commit on 2026-04-06. Adds CSRF fix for public application form, admin franchise-requests review queue, Stripe Checkout Sessions refactor (replaces Payment Links — locks customer email), configurable HIPAA-compliant inactivity timeout, post-checkout success/cancelled landing pages, and the personalized save-the-cancel re-engagement page with practice-type-specific value props. Phase A is now considered fully shipped AND hardened — every customer-facing touchpoint in the franchise launch funnel is verified live in production against real tenants.
shippedCommits: 3f26a27 — Bypass CSRF for public franchise application endpoint (real production bug — no prospect could submit before this)
24e36e5 — Admin franchise application review queue at /admin/franchise-requests + DRY refactor of tenant creation into lib/tenant-create.ts
b68b980 — HIPAA-compliant configurable inactivity timeout (5–30 min, super-admin tunable, NO admin exemption)
e25ad44 — Stripe Checkout Sessions replace Payment Links (locks customer email, prevents the wrong-owner-provisioned bug)
976522b — Polish: brand-clean activation email header + admin tenant edit page subdomain label
da61093 — /franchise/payment-success and /franchise/payment-cancelled landing pages (prevent 404s after Stripe redirect)
6011a0b — Save-the-cancel re-engagement page personalized by practice type with credible reserved-subdomain FOMO + extracted FRANCHISE_PRACTICE_TYPES to shared lib
a714745 — Schedule a Free Demo CTA on cancel page (DRY reuse of existing DemoRequestButton)

changelog.summary: Initial PRD covering Phase A franchise dashboard MVP — tenant subdomains, Stripe webhook activation, magic-link onboarding, branding editor.

product.vision: Sell the 'pipes and wires' that let independent health practitioners (nurses, wellness coaches, concierge doctors, home care agencies, patient advocates) launch a HIPAA-compliant, AI-powered, fully branded family-health platform under their own name in 48 hours — without writing code or buying infrastructure.
product.positioning: A white-label 'business-in-a-box' for the missing middle between off-the-shelf consumer apps (cheap, generic, not BAA-eligible) and custom builds ($100k+, 6-12 months). WPL's franchise tier gives the polish of a custom app at the price point of premium SaaS.
targetUsers: Solo nurses and home-care providers who need a HIPAA-compliant client management platform
Wellness coaches scaling beyond 10 clients without hiring developers
Concierge physicians who want a branded patient portal without enterprise EHR cost
Home care agencies managing multiple caregivers and client families
keyDifferentiators: True white-label — branded subdomain, logo, colors, company name
HIPAA + BAA — most no-code platforms cannot offer this
AI-augmented client management — one provider can manage 50+ families
$3,000 setup fee acts as a quality filter — no hobbyists, only serious practices
Premium boutique pricing tiers ($750–$2,000/mo) align with high-touch services

successMetrics.applicationToActivationTime: median <48 hours from POST /api/franchise/apply to status:paid
successMetrics.paidActivationTime: <5 seconds from Stripe checkout.session.completed to tenant status:paid + activation email
successMetrics.ownerFirstSignInTime: <60 seconds from clicking magic link to landing in /dashboard signed in
successMetrics.subdomainTtfb: <200ms p95 for the branded landing page (statically cached where possible)
successMetrics.applicationConversionRate: >25% of /franchise marketing visitors who reach /franchise/apply submit the form
successMetrics.activationDropoff: <10% of payment-link sends fail to complete payment within 7 days
successMetrics.operationalLoadPerCustomer: <15 minutes/month of admin time per active franchise (Phase A) — drops to <5 minutes once Phase B ships

outOfBandConsiderations: MASTER_PRD.md feature totals (line ~228) currently sum to 287 but the header says 157 — pre-existing inconsistency unrelated to franchise; flagged but not fixed here
docs/UNIFIED_PRD.json is named 'unified' but is actually the onboarding flow config; the franchise reference does NOT belong there

userTypes.label: WPL Super Admin
userTypes.description: Internal staff managing the franchise program. Creates tenants in the back office, sends payment links, monitors activations, can edit any tenant.
userTypes.auth: Firebase Auth + super-admin email allowlist + role custom claim

userTypes.label: Franchise Admin / Owner
userTypes.description: The practitioner who paid the setup fee. Owns one tenant. Self-service editing of branding (Phase A); will eventually invite staff and review managed-family requests.
userTypes.auth: Firebase Auth, currently magic-link only (no password yet)
userTypes.provisionedBy: Stripe webhook on first checkout.session.completed for the tenant

userTypes.label: Franchise Staff
userTypes.description: Nurses, assistants, or coaches employed by the franchise. Invited by the franchise owner. Manage assigned client families. Counts against the tenant's seat allowance.

userTypes.label: Managed Client Family
userTypes.description: An existing WPL consumer family that has chosen to be managed by a franchise. Their data becomes visible to the franchise's staff with permission scoping. Linked via user.managedBy[] containing the tenantId. Family-initiated relationship — never created from a tenant subdomain self-signup.
userTypes.claims: (no tenant claims; relationship lives in user.managedBy[])

Tenant.description: Top-level franchise entity. One document per tenant in the `tenants` Firestore collection. Document id is auto-generated; the `slug` field is the URL-safe subdomain identifier.

schema.slug: string (lowercase a-z0-9-, indexed; the subdomain identifier)
schema.name: string
schema.ownerUid: string (Firebase Auth uid of provisioned owner) — set on first webhook activation

schema.logoUrl: string (hosted image URL; direct upload deferred)
schema.primaryColor: string (BARE HSL triplet, e.g. '262 83% 58%' — must be wrapped as hsl(...) when applied as a CSS color)

TenantBranding.notes: The HSL-triplet storage convention is intentional (Tailwind / shadcn-style) but NOT a valid CSS color on its own. Three render sites currently inline a small toCss() helper: app/tenant-shell/page.tsx, app/tenant-shell/layout.tsx, app/tenant-shell/dashboard/BrandingEditor.tsx. Extracting to lib/tenant-color.ts is the next reuse-driven refactor (rule of three reached).

schema.currentSeats: number (hardcoded 0 today; will auto-increment in Phase B)
schema.paymentLinkUrl: string (set when admin sends payment link)

TenantFeatures.description: Per-tenant feature flags. Currently metadata only (not enforced at API/rules layer); enforcement is Phase C work.

FranchiseApplication.description: Public-facing application form submission. Lives in the `franchise_applications` collection separate from `tenants` so we can track lead funnel without polluting the active-tenant set.

FranchisePlan.description: Single source of truth for the three pricing tiers. Imported by both the marketing page and the application form.

ManagementRequest.description: PLANNED (Phase B). A WPL consumer family asking to be managed by a specific franchise. Created from inside the family's existing dashboard, NOT from the tenant subdomain.

schema.userId: string (Firebase Auth uid of the requesting family head)

states.pending: Tenant doc created via the public application form (/api/franchise/apply) — not yet reviewed by an admin.
states.pending_payment: Admin reviewed and sent the Stripe payment link via /api/admin/tenants/[id]/payment-link. Owner has been emailed but has not yet paid. Subdomain shows the branded 'Confirming your payment' waiting page.
states.paid: Stripe webhook fired and tenant was activated. Owner Firebase Auth account provisioned with custom claims, magic sign-in link sent. Branded subdomain is fully live.
states.active: Equivalent to 'paid' for runtime purposes — accepted as a synonym so back-office tools can flip status manually if needed. tenant-shell layout treats both as 'render normally'.
states.suspended: Admin suspended the tenant (e.g. non-payment, ToS violation). Subdomain shows the 'not currently active' page. Reactivation flips back to 'active'.
states.canceled: Tenant terminated. Same UX as suspended. Data retention follows the franchise agreement.

transitions.trigger: POST /api/admin/tenants (admin manual create) — bypasses payment for internal demo tenants

ownerProvisioning.description: Sequence of operations inside handleFranchiseSetupPaid() when the Stripe webhook fires for a tenant. All steps are idempotent so Stripe retries are safe.
sequence: 2. Idempotency check: if status already paid/active OR billing.setupFeePaid===true → log and return
3. Update tenant: status='paid', billing.setupFeePaid=true, billing.setupFeePaidAt, billing.stripeCheckoutSessionId, updatedAt
4. Look up Firebase Auth user by tenant.contact.adminEmail. If not found → createUser() helper
8. Send single activation email via Resend (sendEmail) — CTA is the magic link, fallback CTA if link generation failed
ownerProvisioning.errorHandling: All provisioning steps are wrapped in try/catch. If steps 4-7 fail, the tenant is still marked paid (step 3 already succeeded) and the activation email is sent with the bare subdomain URL as fallback. Admins can re-trigger from the back office.

userFlows.name: Marketing funnel → application
steps: 1. Prospect lands on apex marketing page wellnessprojectionlab.com
2. Clicks 'Are you a nurse or care provider? License our platform →' footer link
3. Lands on /franchise (static marketing page with hero, pricing, FAQ)
4. Clicks any 'Apply Now' CTA → /franchise/apply (5-step form)
5. Submits form → POST /api/franchise/apply → doc in franchise_applications collection + admin notification email
6. 'Application received' confirmation page

userFlows.name: Admin review → send payment link
steps: 1. Super admin reviews application in /admin/tenants (or directly creates a tenant in /admin/tenants/create for white-glove customers)
2. Tenant doc lands in tenants/ collection with status='pending' or 'active'
3. Admin clicks 'Send payment link' → POST /api/admin/tenants/[id]/payment-link
4. Stripe Price + PaymentLink created on the fly with metadata { tenantId }
5. Branded HTML email sent via Resend with the payment link CTA
6. Tenant flipped to status='pending_payment'

userFlows.name: Payment → automatic activation
steps: 1. Owner clicks payment link in email, lands on Stripe-hosted checkout
2. Pays with card
3. Stripe sends checkout.session.completed webhook to /api/webhooks/stripe
4. Webhook detects session.metadata.tenantId → branches into handleFranchiseSetupPaid
5. See lifecycles.ownerProvisioning sequence above
6. Owner receives 'Sign in to your dashboard' email with magic link
7. Total time from successful charge to email arrival: <5 seconds

userFlows.name: Magic link → first sign-in
steps: 1. Owner clicks magic link in email
2. Lands on https://{slug}.wellnessprojectionlab.com/auth/finish-sign-in?email=... (rewritten via proxy.ts to /tenant-shell/auth/finish-sign-in)
3. Client-side signInWithEmailLink completes the Firebase handshake
4. ID token force-refreshed to pick up tenantId + tenantRole custom claims
5. router.replace('/dashboard') → branded dashboard with the BrandingEditor

userFlows.name: Self-serve branding edit
steps: 1. Owner edits company name / logo URL / primary color (HSL triplet) / support email in BrandingEditor
2. Live HSL preview swatch updates as they type
3. Submit → PATCH /api/tenant/branding with Bearer token
4. Server: verifyTenantAdminAuth → tenant id match check → field sanitization → Firestore update branding.* + updatedAt
5. Audit log entry written via logAdminAction with action='tenant_branding_updated', tenantId, changes
6. revalidatePath('/tenant-shell') so the public landing page reflects the new branding immediately
7. Success toast in editor

userFlows.name: Family requests franchise management
steps: 1. Existing WPL consumer family logged in to apex /dashboard
2. Visits new /find-a-provider directory (filter by practice type, location)
3. Clicks a franchise to view its public profile (read from tenants/{id}, branded preview)
4. Clicks 'Request to be managed by {Company}' → modal with optional message
5. Submit → POST /api/tenant/[id]/management-request → doc in tenantManagementRequests collection
6. Resend email to franchise owner notifying of the new request
7. Family sees 'Request pending' status in their /providers panel

userFlows.name: Owner reviews and approves management request
steps: 1. Owner sees pending requests count in dashboard sidebar
2. Clicks Pending Requests → list view with family name, message, request date
3. Clicks Approve → server appends tenantId to user.managedBy[] + flips request to 'approved' + sends acceptance email
4. Family appears in 'Managed Families' tab
5. Approving increments tenant.billing.currentSeats (counted against the family-seat allowance, not staff seats)

userFlows.name: Owner invites staff member
steps: 1. Owner clicks 'Invite Staff' in dashboard
2. Enters email + role
3. POST /api/tenant/[id]/invitations → doc in tenants/{id}/invitations subcollection (already supported by firestore.rules) + Resend email with one-click activation link
4. Staff clicks link → finish-sign-in flow → claims set { tenantId, tenantRole: 'franchise_staff' } → land in dashboard
5. Increments tenant.billing.currentSeats against the staff allowance

shipped.purpose: Create a tenant directly (admin white-glove path)
Generate Stripe Payment Link, email it, flip status to pending_payment
Handle checkout.session.completed for both consumer subscriptions AND franchise setup-fee payments. Distinguished by session.metadata.tenantId.
Update tenant branding fields (logoUrl, primaryColor, companyName, supportEmail)

planned.purpose: Family requests to be managed by a tenant
Create a Stripe Customer Portal session for self-serve billing

publicApex.purpose: Family-end-user marketing homepage with For Practitioners footer

tenantSubdomain.purpose: Franchise owner dashboard (Phase A: branding only)
Managed families list + pending requests queue
Self-serve billing summary + Stripe portal link

routes.routingMechanism: proxy.ts extractSubdomain → injects x-tenant-slug header → rewrites non-apex paths to /tenant-shell/<path> via NextResponse.rewrite

authentication.ownerAccount: Firebase Auth, magic-link only at first (no password). Account auto-provisioned by Stripe webhook.
authentication.sessionRefresh: After magic link sign-in, the client calls user.getIdToken(true) to force-refresh and pick up the new claims before navigating to /dashboard

authorization.tenantAdmin: lib/tenant-auth.ts verifyTenantAdminAuth — Bearer token → adminAuth.verifyIdToken → check claims.tenantRole === 'franchise_admin' AND claims.tenantId matches the requested tenant. Super admins (claims.role === 'admin') bypass the tenantId check.
authorization.writeBoundary: All tenant writes go through PATCH /api/tenant/branding which sanitizes the payload to ONLY the four allowed branding fields. No privilege escalation by writing arbitrary fields.
authorization.firestoreDefenseInDepth: firestore.rules tenants/{tenantId} write rule: allow write: if isAdmin() || isTenantAdmin(tenantId). The API route is the canonical enforcement point; the rule prevents direct-from-client writes.

tenants/{tenantId}.read: allow read: if true; (public — needed for branded landing page when visitor is anonymous)
tenants/{tenantId}.knownLooseness: allow read: if true is intentional for Phase A but means any authenticated user can enumerate tenant docs. TODO(phase-b) tighten to expose only branding/name/slug/status publicly.

tenants/{tenantId}/invitations/{invitati.read: allow read: if isAdmin() || (isAuthenticated() && request.auth.token.tenantId == tenantId);
tenants/{tenantId}/invitations/{invitati.write: allow write: if isAdmin() || (isAuthenticated() && request.auth.token.tenantRole == 'franchise_admin' && request.auth.token.tenantId == tenantId);
tenants/{tenantId}/invitations/{invitati.status: rules already in place from a prior session; staff invitation flow that uses them is Phase B

hipaa.BAA: Signed with every franchise partner. Document at /baa.
hipaa.encryption: Firestore at rest + TLS in transit (Firebase default, sufficient for BAA-eligibility on Google Cloud).
hipaa.dataIsolation: PHASE A LIMITATION: managed-family data isolation is not yet enforced at the rules layer because the management-request flow does not exist. Phase C work. Today, all consumer family data lives in user-owned Firestore docs gated by isOwner(userId); franchises cannot read it because no staff accounts exist yet.
hipaa.auditLogging: All tenant-affecting actions (super admin and franchise owner) write to admin_audit_logs via logAdminAction. tenantId is now an indexed field on the log doc for queryability.

phaseA.label: Phase A — Make the 48-Hour Promise True (+ hardening)
deliverables: Tenant subdomain routing via proxy.ts
Branded tenant landing page (sign-in only, no public account creation)
Branded 'payment processing' waiting page for pending_payment status
Stripe webhook auto-activates franchise on checkout.session.completed
Owner Firebase Auth account auto-provisioned with tenantRole custom claim
Magic sign-in link emailed via Resend
Magic link finish page on the tenant subdomain
Branded /dashboard with self-serve branding editor (logo, color, name, support email)
PATCH /api/tenant/branding with auth + sanitization + audit logging + revalidatePath
firestore.rules isTenantAdmin helper + tenant write rule widened to franchise admins
Static franchise marketing page at /franchise + relocated 5-step form at /franchise/apply
lib/franchise-plans.ts as single source of truth for plan tiers + setup fee
phaseAHardeningShipped: CSRF bypass for /api/franchise/* — fixed silent 403 that was blocking ALL public application submissions (commit 3f26a27)
/admin/franchise-requests review queue: list page + filter tabs + detail page + Approve/Reject/Mark Reviewed actions, all backed by GET/PATCH/POST APIs (commit 24e36e5)

lib/tenant-create.ts shared helper — 130 lines of tenant doc shaping extracted from /api/admin/tenants/route.ts so both the manual create form AND the franchise-requests Approve flow share the same source of truth (commit 24e36e5)

Configurable inactivity timeout — runtime platform setting at admin/platform_settings, super-admin tunable from /admin/settings, range 5–30 min, default 15. NO admin exemption (HIPAA Security Rule § 164.312(a)(2)(iii) compliance — privileged sessions get the same timeout as regular users). lib/platform-settings.ts (server-safe constants/validator) + hooks/usePlatformSettings.ts (client React hook with onSnapshot for real-time propagation) (commit b68b980)
/api/admin/settings/inactivity API (GET + PATCH) with compliance bound enforcement and audit-logged change tracking (commit b68b980)
firestore.rules: new admin/platform_settings rule before the general /admin/{docId} rule — readable by any authenticated user, writable only by super-admins (commit b68b980)

Stripe Checkout Sessions REPLACE Payment Links for franchise setup fee. New flow: get-or-create stripe.customers + stripe.checkout.sessions.create with `customer: <id>` (locks email field read-only on Stripe checkout page). Fixes a real bug where Payment Links let customers type any email at checkout, which would have provisioned the wrong owner via the webhook. Webhook compatibility unchanged — same metadata.tenantId on session.completed event. (commit e25ad44)
Stripe Customer record persisted as tenant.billing.stripeCustomerId — Phase C recurring billing will reuse this
checkout session expires_at = 24 hours so old un-paid links auto-expire
Activation email subject + footer brand-cleaned: 'Welcome to the Wellness Projection Lab Family' (was 'WPL Family')
Admin tenant edit page subdomain label brand-cleaned: '.wellnessprojectionlab.com' (was '.wpl.com')
/franchise/payment-success and /franchise/payment-cancelled landing pages — replaced 404s after Stripe redirects with branded, tenant-personalized confirmation/recovery pages (commit da61093)

Save-the-cancel re-engagement page at /franchise/payment-cancelled — personalized 'Why {practice-type}s choose WPL' value-prop block keyed by tenant.practiceType, credible reserved-subdomain FOMO banner ({slug}.wellnessprojectionlab.com reserved for 24h), and a Schedule-a-Free-Demo CTA reusing the existing DemoRequestButton modal pattern (commits 6011a0b + a714745)

FRANCHISE_PRACTICE_TYPES extracted to lib/franchise-plans.ts — third caller (cancel page) reached the rule of three, so the canonical list now lives in a shared module imported by the apply form, the cancel page, and the type union
lib/tenant-server.ts: new getTenantById() helper mirroring getTenantBySlug() — used by post-checkout pages which receive the Firestore doc id from Stripe redirects, not the slug

End-to-end production validation: real franchise application from 'Little Care Bears' submitted via the form, reviewed via /admin/franchise-requests, approved → tenant doc → 'Send Payment Link' → branded Stripe checkout session with locked email → tenant subdomain renders branded 'pending_payment' waiting page. The entire customer-facing funnel is verified live.

phaseB.label: Phase B — Make the 'AI-Augmented Multi-Client' Promise True
deliverables: Family-side /find-a-provider directory (apex)
POST /api/tenant/[id]/management-request from inside family dashboard
Franchise dashboard 'Pending Requests' queue + approve/decline UI
user.managedBy[] field + Firestore rules update for staff-can-read-managed-families
Franchise dashboard 'Managed Families' list view
Staff invitation flow: POST /api/tenant/[id]/invitations + acceptance page + claim setting
currentSeats automatic increment/decrement (split into family-seats and staff-seats counters)
Tenant public profile page on the subdomain (currently the landing page IS the only public page)
openQuestions: Can a family be managed by multiple franchises simultaneously? Recommendation: NO for MVP (single tenantId in user.managedBy, not array). Relax later if needed.
What happens to a family's existing data when a franchise gets revoked? Recommendation: family retains everything; tenant loses access immediately.

phaseC.label: Phase C — Recurring Billing + Hardened Security
deliverables: Stripe Subscription Products + Prices for the three plan tiers (6 prices: starter/pro/enterprise × monthly/annual)
Subscription auto-creation on tenant activation
Stripe Customer Portal link in dashboard for self-serve plan changes
Per-seat usage metering (additional staff/families above plan allowance auto-billed)
Failed payment handling + dunning emails
Tenant-scoped Firestore rules for managed-family data — staff of tenant X can read user data only when tenantId in user.managedBy
Tighten tenants/{id} read rule to expose only public branding fields publicly
Branded transactional emails (currently the activation email has tenant name but not tenant colors)
Branded /login page (apex login currently shows WPL branding even when accessed from a tenant subdomain)

phaseD.label: Phase D — Polish + Scale
deliverables: Direct logo file upload (Cloudinary or Firebase Storage)
Custom domain support (e.g. care.maria-rn.com instead of maria.wellnessprojectionlab.com)
Branding editor: secondary color, accent color, font choices, custom CSS escape hatch
Branded mobile push notifications
Per-tenant analytics dashboard
Tenant-side audit log viewer ('who edited what')
Multi-language support per tenant
ROI calculator on /franchise marketing page
Real testimonials (currently placeholder)

outOfScope: WPL operating its own clinical practice — WPL sells infrastructure only, not health advice
Replacing electronic health record systems (EHR) — franchises that need full EHR integration are referred to enterprise vendors
Direct-to-consumer franchise marketing (Facebook ads, etc.) — franchises must do their own family acquisition; WPL provides the infrastructure and the family-side directory in Phase B
Per-tenant deployment isolation — all tenants share one Next.js app, one Firestore project, one Stripe account. Multi-region or per-tenant infra is out of scope indefinitely.
Custom feature development for individual franchises — feature flags are per-tenant but new features ship to all tenants together

openQuestions.question: Should monthly recurring use Stripe Subscriptions (cleaner reporting) or stay on the dynamic Price-creation pattern that the setup fee uses (zero dashboard setup)?
openQuestions.currentLeaning: Subscriptions — six pre-created Prices in env vars give clean MRR reporting in Stripe dashboard. Worth the 10-minute setup. Note: stripeCustomerId is now persisted on the tenant doc as of e25ad44, which is the prerequisite for subscription creation.

openQuestions.question: Do owners ever need to set a password, or is magic-link-forever sufficient?
openQuestions.currentLeaning: Add a 'Set password' option in dashboard Settings tab once Phase A is in production. Magic link covers the first ~10 sign-ins per owner reliably.

openQuestions.question: Can a family be managed by multiple franchises at once?
openQuestions.currentLeaning: No for MVP. Single tenantId in user.managedBy[]. Relax later if data shows demand.

openQuestions.question: Should tenant subdomains be indexed by Google? Today they all return tenant content but the apex marketing site is the canonical brand.
openQuestions.currentLeaning: noindex tenant subdomains for Phase A — they're for direct visitors only. Phase B may need a /robots policy per tenant once franchises start sharing their own URLs.

openQuestions.question: When a family approves franchise management, what consent record is required for HIPAA?
openQuestions.currentLeaning: Click-through consent stored in tenantManagementRequests doc with timestamp + IP + user agent. Phase B work.

openQuestions.question: The save-the-cancel page tells prospects 'Your subdomain is reserved for you for 24 hours' but we don't actually have a cron that releases held slugs after 24h — admins would need to manually release them. Should we build the auto-release cron, or accept the soft promise as-is?
openQuestions.currentLeaning: Build the cron in Phase B alongside the management-request flow. Until then the copy is honest because admins CAN manually release a held slug if a serious applicant comes along — we just don't guarantee the timing.

openQuestions.question: The current save-the-cancel page has the right structure (reassurance + reserved-subdomain FOMO + personalized value prop + demo CTA + return-to-checkout) but the copy is functional rather than emotionally compelling. User feedback was 'this does not inspire too much.' Worth a copy-only redesign pass to make it feel like a confident company talking to a hesitant buyer, not a status report from a database?
openQuestions.currentLeaning: Yes — but with the constraint that the new narrative must be sourced FROM THIS PRD's positioning ('the missing middle' between off-the-shelf consumer apps and custom builds at $100k+/6-12 months) and targetUsers entries, not invented from scratch. The PRD is the source of truth for the cost-anchor and pain points. NEXT WORK ITEM after this PRD update commits.

code.paymentCancelledPage: app/franchise/payment-cancelled/page.tsx (save-the-cancel layout with practice-type personalization, reserved-subdomain FOMO banner, and DemoRequestButton CTA)
code.tenantCreate: lib/tenant-create.ts (DRY shared helper used by both manual /admin/tenants/create AND franchise-requests Approve flow)
code.platformSettings: lib/platform-settings.ts (server-safe: types, defaults, validator, compliance bounds)
code.platformSettingsHook: hooks/usePlatformSettings.ts (client React hook with onSnapshot real-time propagation)
code.demoRequestButton: components/DemoRequestButton.tsx + components/DemoRequestModal.tsx (REUSED from /blog/* by the cancel page — no new modal infrastructure)
code.paymentLinkApi: app/api/admin/tenants/[tenantId]/payment-link/route.ts (uses Checkout Sessions, NOT Payment Links — locks customer email)
code.platformSettingsApi: app/api/admin/settings/inactivity/route.ts (GET + PATCH, super-admin only, validates compliance bounds)
code.auditLog: lib/admin/audit.ts (extended with tenantId field + tenant_*, franchise_application_*, platform_setting_updated actions)
code.firestoreRules: firestore.rules (isTenantAdmin helper + tenants write rule + admin/platform_settings rule)

docs.legacyPlatformPRD: docs/PRD.md (overlapping with MASTER_PRD.md — pre-existing duplication, not addressed by this PRD)

knownDebt.description: The toCss(hslOrHex) helper now lives in 3 files: app/tenant-shell/page.tsx, app/tenant-shell/layout.tsx, app/tenant-shell/dashboard/BrandingEditor.tsx. Rule of three reached. Extract to lib/tenant-color.ts.
firestore.rules tenants/{id} allow read: if true exposes all tenant fields including billing/contact/EIN. Tighten in Phase B/C.
tenant.billing.currentSeats is hardcoded to 0 and never updated. Breaks billing math at customer #2. Fix in Phase B alongside staff invitations.
MASTER_PRD.md and docs/PRD.md both claim to be the live platform PRD. Pick one. Not addressed by this franchise PRD.
MASTER_PRD.md header says 157 features but the category summary table sums to 287 (now 299 after adding the franchise category row). Header value is stale.

types/tenant.ts Tenant interface is missing many fields that lib/tenant-create.ts actually writes: legalName, entityType, ein, stateOfIncorporation, licenseNumber, npiNumber, staffCount, familyCount, expectedLaunchDate, leadSource, notes, emergencyContact, billingAddress, ownerUid, ownerProvisionedAt. Only practiceType was added (commit 6011a0b) as the minimum to compile the cancel page. Worth a focused type-completion pass.

The 'Admin Users' panel on /admin/settings shows 'No admin users found' even though super-admins exist. Likely cause: the /api/admin/settings/admin-users endpoint queries Firestore users/{uid}.role === 'admin' but super-admins are recognized by email allowlist in lib/admin/permissions.ts, so they don't have a Firestore role field. Cosmetic — doesn't affect access control, just the display panel.

The 'Recent Admin Activity' panel on /admin/settings shows 'No audit logs found' even when fresh entries exist (verified after saving an inactivity timeout change). Cause TBD — likely a query / serialization issue in /api/admin/settings/audit-logs. Cosmetic — audit logs ARE being written correctly to admin_audit_logs collection.

Browser console shows manifest.json 404 on tenant subdomains because proxy.ts rewrites /manifest.json into /tenant-shell/manifest.json, which doesn't exist. PWA features (install prompt, service worker) are broken on tenant subdomains. Fix: either generate a tenant-aware manifest at app/tenant-shell/manifest.ts OR add /manifest.json to proxy.ts apex-only allowlist.

On tenant subdomains, Next.js prefetches for /blog/*, /pricing, /docs, /referrals (apex marketing pages) all 404 because they're rewritten into /tenant-shell/* paths that don't exist. Caused by a shared component that includes <Link href='/blog/...'> tags rendered inside the tenant-shell layout. Fix: figure out which footer/nav component is leaking and either render a different one for tenant subdomains, or use plain <a> tags for cross-host links.

support@wellnessprojectionlab.com is referenced in the activation email body but no real inbox exists at that address — replies would bounce. Fix: set up Cloudflare Email Routing (free) to forward support@ → wellnessprojectionlab@gmail.com. ~10 min, no infrastructure cost.
